perturbation.original_text: The Company undertakes to consider as confidential any confidential, private, inte rnal information, trade secret or other classified information, materials or samples that The Company receives, sees, hears or that it studies in writing or learns about in some other way in connection with the Project, and that relates to the Client or an y of its affiliates or subsidiaries, irrespective of whether such information, such materials or samples were received from the Client, its affiliates or subsidiaries or from third parties on the Client’s instructions (hereinafter the “Confidential Informa tion”). All information obtained through a statement, processing, generalizations or analytical calculations from the Confidential Information is also confidential .
perturbation.changed_text: The Company undertakes to consider as confidential any confidential, private, inte rnal information, trade secret or other classified information, materials or samples that The Company receives, sees, hears or that it studies in writing or learns about in some other way in connection with the Project, and that relates to the Client or an y of its affiliates or subsidiaries, irrespective of whether such information, such materials or samples were received from the Client, its affiliates or subsidiaries or from third parties on the Client’s instructions (hereinafter the “Confidential Informa tion”). All information obtained through a statement, processing, generalizations or analytical calculations from the Confidential Information may, at the Company's discretion, be considered confidential .
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'may, at the Company's discretion, be considered confidential,' the originally binding definition of Confidential Information in Section 1 becomes discretionary, contradicting the later sections that assume all such information is inherently confidential. This is an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: HOW THE COMPANY SHOULD PROTECT THE CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION The Company undertakes to act in good faith for the purpose of protecting the confidentiality of the Confidential Information. This undertaking means that (а) The Company undertakes to disclose the Confidential Information solely in the instances permitted by sections 5 and 6 of this Agreement or with the preliminary written consent of the Client; b) The Company undertakes to use the Confidential Information solely in the instances when this is required in connection with the Project, and not to use it in any other way fo r the purpose of deriving its own benefit; and (c) The Company undertakes to take all reasonable measures for the purpose of preventing the unintentional disclosure of the Confidential Information. The Company undertakes to report attempts by unauthorized persons to obtain the Confidential Information, the loss or shortfall in the media containing such information, and also other facts that could impair the activities of the Client, and also attempts by third parties to use or disclose the Confidential Information within 2 (Two) working days of the actual receipt of the information on these attempts.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines Confidential Information with certainty. The changed text modifies the definition, making whether information derived from Confidential Information is considered confidential discretionary ('may, at the Company's discretion, be considered confidential'). This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which outlines mandatory actions the Company 'undertakes' to protect the confidentiality of the Confidential Information. If the Company has the discretion to decide whether information is confidential, the obligations to protect it, as described in the 'Contradicted Text', become unenforceable or ambiguous, creating a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the provided definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Information does not constitute Confi dential Information if: (а) it is in the public domain or enters the public domain through no fault of The Company ; (b) it was known to The Company before it was provided by the Client, which is confirmed by The Company ’s documents; (c) it was disclosed to The Company by a third party that has not assumed any non -disclosure undertakings before the Client, which is confirmed by The Company ’s documents or the documents of said third party; or (d) it was independently devel oped by The Company without the use of the Confidential Information, which is confirmed by The Company ’s documents. The Client is not required to consider as confidential any information provided by The Company under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Information does not constitute Confi dential Information if: (а) it is in the public domain or enters the public domain through no fault of The Company ; (b) it was known to The Company before it was provided by the Client; (c) it was disclosed to The Company by a third party that has not assumed any non -disclosure undertakings before the Client; or (d) it was independently devel oped by The Company without the use of the Confidential Information. The Client is required to consider as confidential any information provided by The Company under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the requirement that the Company's prior knowledge or third-party disclosure needs to be 'confirmed by The Company's documents' and adding 'The Client is required to consider as confidential any information provided by The Company', we remove an important condition of what is considered Confidential Information for the Company, and change it that the client must, creating an in-text contradiction and affecting the ability of the Company to defend itself against claims.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHAT IS THE LIABILITY OF THE SUPPLIER FOR VIOLATION OF THE TERMS AND CONDITIONS OF THIS AGREEMENT The Company is fully aware that the Client could incur losses as a result of the violation of this Agreement. Consequen tly, in the event of the disclosure of information related to the Confidential Information, THE COMPANY undertakes liability for any real damage suffered by the Client, and occurred directly by disclosure of Confidential Information on behalf of THE COMPAN Y in violation of this Agreement, however, the amount of reimbursement will be defined in accordance with the Consulting Services Agreement , in relation to which Confidential Information was provided by the Client. Under no circumstances THE COMPANY shall reimburse the Client against lost profit and consequential damages. In order to receive reimbursement of real damage, suffered by the Client in connection to this Agreement, the Client shall provide THE COMPANY with documents and/or other proof, confirming the presence and amount of real damage.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed the Company to use its own documents to confirm prior knowledge or third-party disclosure, defining what qualifies as confidential information. The changed text removes this requirement and imposes a new obligation on the Client to treat all information from the Company as confidential, which is a direct contradiction to the original agreement regarding Confidential Information. The contradicted text holds the Company liable for disclosure of confidential information, but the changed text expands the definition of what the Client must consider confidential, thus broadening the Company's potential liability and contradicting the original intent of limited liability based on specific documented conditions. This causes an in-text contradiction as it changes the scope of liability of the Supplier from the Contradicted Text.

perturbation.original_text: The Company undertakes to act in good faith for the purpose of protecting the confidentiality of the Confidential Information. This undertaking means that (а) The Company undertakes to disclose the Confidential Information solely in the instances permitted by sections 5 and 6 of this Agreement or with the preliminary written consent of the Client; b) The Company undertakes to use the Confidential Information solely in the instances when this is required in connection with the Project, and not to use it in any other way fo r the purpose of deriving its own benefit; and (c) The Company undertakes to take all reasonable measures for the purpose of preventing the unintentional disclosure of the Confidential Information. The Company undertakes to report attempts by unauthorized persons to obtain the Confidential Information, the loss or shortfall in the media containing such information, and also other facts that could impair the activities of the Client, and also attempts by third parties to use or disclose the Confidential Information within 2 (Two) working days of the actual receipt of the information on these attempts.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company undertakes to act in good faith for the purpose of protecting the confidentiality of the Confidential Information, except when directed by a court order. This undertaking means that (а) The Company undertakes to disclose the Confidential Information solely in the instances permitted by sections 5 and 6 of this Agreement or with the preliminary written consent of the Client; b) The Company undertakes to use the Confidential Information solely in the instances when this is required in connection with the Project, and not to use it in any other way fo r the purpose of deriving its own benefit; and (c) The Company undertakes to take all reasonable measures for the purpose of preventing the unintentional disclosure of the Confidential Information. The Company undertakes to report attempts by unauthorized persons to obtain the Confidential Information, the loss or shortfall in the media containing such information, and also other facts that could impair the activities of the Client, and also attempts by third parties to use or disclose the Confidential Information within 2 (Two) working days of the actual receipt of the information on these attempts.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'except when directed by a court order' to the initial commitment to confidentiality, while section 6 already addresses court-ordered disclosure, this creates ambiguity. It's unclear if 'except when directed by a court order' refers ONLY to the scenario already covered in section 6, or allows the Company to proactively seek a court order to disclose information, thereby weakening the protection of confidentiality and making an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEN THE COMPANY IS ENTITLED TO DISCLOSE CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION TO THIRD PARTIES If The Company is required to disclose the Confidential Information in accordance with a court decision or resolution of the state authority, it should notify the Client thereof as soon as possible (other than instances where The Company by law may not provide such notice). The Company shall only have the right to disclose such Confidential Information in the scope required for the performance of this decision or resolution. At the same time, the Confidential Information remains Confidential Information protected in full by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text provides an unconditional undertaking to protect confidential information. The changed text introduces an exception ('except when directed by a court order') that weakens this undertaking. While the contradicted text (section on court-ordered disclosure) allows for disclosure under specific court orders, the added exception in the changed text creates ambiguity. It's unclear whether the Company can proactively seek a court order to disclose information outside the scope of the original agreement, thus creating an in-text contradiction because it alters the original agreement to disclose information to third parties. Therefore, it is a contradiction because it deviates from the original parameters in a way that could be used to circumvent the agreement. This aligns with the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, where a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: The Company undertakes to provide access to the Confidential Information solely: (а) to its employees who require access to the Confidential Information to perform the Project; and (b) to its authorized representatives and subcontractors, who (i) require access to the Confidential Information to implement the Project, and who (ii) had been approve d in advance in writing by the Client to perform the work on the Project, and (iii) have signed non -disclosure agreements, reflecting the actual transfer of such Confidential Information and establishing the procedure for its use, at the very least on term s and conditions analogous to the terms and conditions of this Agreement. The Company undertakes to provide access to the Confidential Information solely to THE COMPANY representative s, as they are defined below, for the purpose of conducting negotiations, contract preparation and taking part in the work under the Contract for the Client, as well as reviewing the quality of procedures, and THE COMPANY professional consultants on confide ntial terms and conditions analogous to terms and conditions of this Agreement , for the purpose of THE COMPANY getting necessary consultation in connection with the implementation of the Project . At the same time, “THE COMPANY Representatives” – are THE CO MPANY as the Party under this Agreement; all member firm s of the network affiliated with THE COMPANY International Cooperative and all controlled by such member firms individuals, including all partners, directors, employees and agents of such member firms and controlled by such individuals , and “THE COMPANY Representative” means any of the mentioned above. THE COMPANY ensures that every THE COMPANY Representative and THE COMPANY professional consultant, having access to the Confidential Information above, is informed about terms and conditions of this Agreement and complied with them. Further to the request of the Client, The Company within 5 days should submit a report on the access of third parties to the Confidential Information, including the surname o f the employee/name of the counterparty, the materials studied thereby, the grounds for the need to study them, the date and time of receipt and return of such materials. The Company is liable for the compliance by said individuals with confidentiality req uirements.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company undertakes to provide access to the Confidential Information solely: (а) to its employees who require access to the Confidential Information to perform the Project; and (b) to its authorized representatives and subcontractors. The Company undertakes to provide access to the Confidential Information solely to THE COMPANY representative s, as they are defined below, for the purpose of conducting negotiations, contract preparation and taking part in the work under the Contract for the Client, as well as reviewing the quality of procedures, and THE COMPANY professional consultants on confide ntial terms and conditions analogous to terms and conditions of this Agreement , for the purpose of THE COMPANY getting necessary consultation in connection with the implementation of the Project . At the same time, “THE COMPANY Representatives” – are THE CO MPANY as the Party under this Agreement; all member firm s of the network affiliated with THE COMPANY International Cooperative and all controlled by such member firms individuals, including all partners, directors, employees and agents of such member firms and controlled by such individuals , and “THE COMPANY Representative” means any of the mentioned above. THE COMPANY ensures that every THE COMPANY Representative and THE COMPANY professional consultant, having access to the Confidential Information above, is informed about terms and conditions of this Agreement and complied with them. Further to the request of the Client, The Company within 5 days should submit a report on the access of third parties to the Confidential Information, including the surname o f the employee/name of the counterparty, the materials studied thereby, the grounds for the need to study them, the date and time of receipt and return of such materials. The Company is liable for the compliance by said individuals with confidentiality req uirements.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the requirements that the subcontractors (i) require access to the Confidential Information to implement the Project, and who (ii) had been approved in advance in writing by the Client to perform the work on the Project, and (iii) have signed non -disclosure agreements, reflecting the actual transfer of such Confidential Information and establishing the procedure for its use, at the very least on term s and conditions analogous to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, it makes it vague if they are needed or not. Thus creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHAT IS THE LIABILITY OF THE SUPPLIER FOR VIOLATION OF THE TERMS AND CONDITIONS OF THIS AGREEMENT The Company is fully aware that the Client could incur losses as a result of the violation of this Agreement. Consequen tly, in the event of the disclosure of information related to the Confidential Information, THE COMPANY undertakes liability for any real damage suffered by the Client, and occurred directly by disclosure of Confidential Information on behalf of THE COMPAN Y in violation of this Agreement, however, the amount of reimbursement will be defined in accordance with the Consulting Services Agreement , in relation to which Confidential Information was provided by the Client. Under no circumstances THE COMPANY shall reimburse the Client against lost profit and consequential damages. In order to receive reimbursement of real damage, suffered by the Client in connection to this Agreement, the Client shall provide THE COMPANY with documents and/or other proof, confirming the presence and amount of real damage.
perturbation.justification: The original text imposes specific requirements on subcontractors accessing confidential information: they must (i) require access, (ii) be approved in writing, and (iii) sign NDAs. The changed text removes these requirements. The 'Contradicted Text' specifies that 'THE COMPANY undertakes liability for any real damage suffered by the Client, and occurred directly by disclosure of Confidential Information on behalf of THE COMPANY in violation of this Agreement'. By removing the safeguards related to subcontractors, the modified text increases the risk of unauthorized disclosure, which could trigger liability under the 'Contradicted Text'. The contradiction lies in that with the original text, the company has a tighter leash on the parties that could have access to the data. Whereas in the change text, there is no leash at all. Thus, the modified text creates an in-text contradiction by weakening the controls on confidential information, potentially conflicting with the liability clause.

perturbation.original_text: HOW LONG THE COMPANY SHOULD ENSURE THE CONFIDENTIALITY OF THE CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION The Company should protect the Confidential Information for five years since the Commencement Date of the Provision of the Confidential Information, or (if the Project lasts more than one year) for five years after the completion of the Project. In addition, the Clie nt shall have the right to indicate to The Company in writing any Confidential Information that it deems a trade secret. In this instance The Company undertakes to honor the confidentiality of said Confidential Information for an unlimited term or at the v ery least for as long as such Confidential Information remains a trade secret.
perturbation.changed_text: HOW LONG THE COMPANY SHOULD ENSURE THE CONFIDENTIALITY OF THE CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION The Company should protect the Confidential Information for one year since the Commencement Date of the Provision of the Confidential Information, or (if the Project lasts more than one year) for five years after the completion of the Project. In addition, the Clie nt shall have the right to indicate to The Company in writing any Confidential Information that it deems a trade secret. In this instance The Company is not required to honor the confidentiality of said Confidential Information for an unlimited term or at the v ery least for as long as such Confidential Information remains a trade secret.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the protection period from 'five years' to 'one year' and changing The Company's undertaking to one where it's not required , while keeping the provision for an unlimited term for trade secrets, creates confusion. The original text implied that ALL confidential information is protected for at least five years, with trade secrets possibly longer. Now, it suggests that non-trade secret information might only be protected for a year, making enforcement inconsistent and contradicts the previous commitment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHAT IS THE LIABILITY OF THE SUPPLIER FOR VIOLATION OF THE TERMS AND CONDITIONS OF THIS AGREEMENT The Company is fully aware that the Client could incur losses as a result of the violation of this Agreement. Consequen tly, in the event of the disclosure of information related to the Confidential Information, THE COMPANY undertakes liability for any real damage suffered by the Client, and occurred directly by disclosure of Confidential Information on behalf of THE COMPAN Y in violation of this Agreement, however, the amount of reimbursement will be defined in accordance with the Consulting Services Agreement , in relation to which Confidential Information was provided by the Client. Under no circumstances THE COMPANY shall reimburse the Client against lost profit and consequential damages. In order to receive reimbursement of real damage, suffered by the Client in connection to this Agreement, the Client shall provide THE COMPANY with documents and/or other proof, confirming the presence and amount of real damage.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the confidentiality period from 'five years' to 'one year' for regular confidential information and removes the requirement to maintain confidentiality for trade secrets indefinitely. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which states that 'THE COMPANY undertakes liability for any real damage suffered by the Client... in the event of the disclosure of information related to the Confidential Information.' The shorter confidentiality period and the removal of the indefinite term for trade secrets significantly weaken the protection afforded to the Client's confidential information, creating a clear conflict regarding liability for disclosure.

perturbation.original_text: WHAT IS THE LIABILITY OF THE SUPPLIER FOR VIOLATION OF THE TERMS AND CONDITIONS OF THIS AGREEMENT The Company is fully aware that the Client could incur losses as a result of the violation of this Agreement. Consequen tly, in the event of the disclosure of information related to the Confidential Information, THE COMPANY undertakes liability for any real damage suffered by the Client, and occurred directly by disclosure of Confidential Information on behalf of THE COMPAN Y in violation of this Agreement, however, the amount of reimbursement will be defined in accordance with the Consulting Services Agreement , in relation to which Confidential Information was provided by the Client. Under no circumstances THE COMPANY shall reimburse the Client against lost profit and consequential damages. In order to receive reimbursement of real damage, suffered by the Client in connection to this Agreement, the Client shall provide THE COMPANY with documents and/or other proof, confirming the presence and amount of real damage.
perturbation.changed_text: WHAT IS THE LIABILITY OF THE SUPPLIER FOR VIOLATION OF THE TERMS AND CONDITIONS OF THIS AGREEMENT The Company may not be fully aware that the Client could incur losses as a result of the violation of this Agreement. Consequen tly, in the event of the disclosure of information related to the Confidential Information, THE COMPANY may not be liable for any real damage suffered by the Client, and occurred directly by disclosure of Confidential Information on behalf of THE COMPAN Y in violation of this Agreement, however, the amount of reimbursement will be defined in accordance with the Consulting Services Agreement , in relation to which Confidential Information was provided by the Client. Under no circumstances THE COMPANY shall reimburse the Client against lost profit and consequential damages. In order to receive reimbursement of real damage, suffered by the Client in connection to this Agreement, the Client shall provide THE COMPANY with documents and/or other proof, confirming the presence and amount of real damage.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'is fully aware' to 'may not be fully aware' and 'undertakes liability' to 'may not be liable' directly contradicts the intention of this section, which is to establish liability. While the amount is determined by the Consulting Services Agreement, this change introduces uncertainty about whether the Company will even be held responsible, making it an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: HOW LONG THE COMPANY SHOULD ENSURE THE CONFIDENTIALITY OF THE CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION The Company should protect the Confidential Information for five years since the Commencement Date of the Provision of the Confidential Information, or (if the Project lasts more than one year) for five years after the completion of the Project. In addition, the Clie nt shall have the right to indicate to The Company in writing any Confidential Information that it deems a trade secret. In this instance The Company undertakes to honor the confidentiality of said Confidential Information for an unlimited term or at the v ery least for as long as such Confidential Information remains a trade secret.
perturbation.justification: The original text firmly establishes the company's liability for confidential information disclosure. The changed text introduces uncertainty ('may not be fully aware,' 'may not be liable'). This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which states, 'The Company undertakes to honor the confidentiality'. 'Undertakes to honor' implies a definite obligation, while the changed text introduces a possibility of no liability, thus creating a direct and strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition because the company's liability is addressed in multiple sections with conflicting implications.
